description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ A line of SGDs known for their durability and powerful language software, built on an Android platform. While many competitors differentiate on hardware, PRC-Saltillo’s legacy is rooted in linguistics and motor planning. Systems like Unity and LAMP are designed so that button locations never move, allowing users to build muscle memory for language and move beyond simple requests into complex, novel grammar.
target: Individuals with limited or no speech, including those with autism and intellectual disabilities.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ An iOS-based SGD built on an iPad, offering various access methods including head tracking. While many competitors differentiate on hardware, PRC-Saltillo’s legacy is rooted in linguistics and motor planning. Systems like Unity and LAMP are designed so that button locations never move, allowing users to build muscle memory for language and move beyond simple requests into complex, novel grammar.
target: Children and adults with difficulty using their hands for access.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ A durable, dedicated SGD that runs on Windows and offers the LAMP Words for Life language system. While many competitors differentiate on hardware, PRC-Saltillo’s legacy is rooted in linguistics and motor planning. Systems like Unity and LAMP are designed so that button locations never move, allowing users to build muscle memory for language and move beyond simple requests into complex, novel grammar. Eye-tracking and Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC)
target: Individuals with autism and other communication challenges.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ A medical-grade, eye-controlled communication device designed for durability and performance. Historically, AAC devices were clunky, standalone medical hardware. New devices like TD Pilot and Grid Pad represent a shift toward mainstreamed accessibility: medical-grade eye tracking, mounts and durability wrapped around a familiar tablet form factor so users can 'look like everyone else' while accessing powerful assistive features.
target: Individuals with physical disabilities, including cerebral palsy, ALS, and Rett syndrome, who can use eye-gaze.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ An eye-controlled communication device built into a rugged case for the Apple iPad Pro. Historically, AAC devices were clunky, standalone medical hardware. New devices like TD Pilot and Grid Pad represent a shift toward mainstreamed accessibility: medical-grade eye tracking, mounts and durability wrapped around a familiar tablet form factor so users can 'look like everyone else' while accessing powerful assistive features.
target: Individuals who can benefit from eye-gaze access on a mainstream tablet.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ A portable and durable Windows tablet designed specifically for AAC.

The **SC Tablet** (and its standalone counterpart, the **Speech Case**) answers a critical demand in the adult autism community: the need for a device that offers the social acceptability of an Apple iPad while providing the durability and audio power of a dedicated medical device. While standard consumer tablets are often too fragile for behavioral dysregulation and too quiet for noisy vocational environments, the SC Tablet wraps an iPad in a ruggedized, drop-tested shell featuring powerful integrated speakers. This ensures that a user’s voice is projected clearly across a room, a vital facto
target: A wide range of individuals needing touch-based AAC support in a rugged form factor.

description: __Software __ __ A symbol-based communication software designed to be easy to use and customize, with various page sets available.

product: The Ecosystem of Autonomy: More Than Just Speech
description: **TD Snap** (formerly Snap Core First) has evolved from a simple symbol-based app into a comprehensive platform for adult independence. Recognizing that adults with profound autism require efficient, low-fatigue communication, the software now centers on the **TD Snap Motor Plan** page sets (introduced to compete with systems like LAMP). These layouts rely on distinct motor patterns for every word, allowing users to build muscle memory for vocabulary rather than constantly visually scanning for categories—a critical shift for long-term literacy and rapid conversation. Beyond communication, the
target: Communicators of all ages and abilities, including those with autism, intellectual disabilities, and aphasia.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ A customizable AAC device built on an Apple iPad, offering a choice of popular communication apps in a durable case.

product: The Democratization of Voice: A Flexible Ecosystem
description: The **QuickTalker Freestyle** (often referred to as the QuickTalker Freestyle 2.0 or Mini) fundamentally disrupts the traditional AAC market by acknowledging that a “one-size-fits-all” dedicated device rarely serves a user for life. Instead of locking families into a proprietary operating system, AbleNet pairs a standard Apple iPad (Mini or Pro) with a rugged, amplified case and allows the user to select virtually **any AAC app** from the App Store—including _Proloquo2Go_ , _LAMP Words for Life_ , _TouchChat_ , or _TD Snap_. Uniquely, this “open ecosystem” model permits the software to be **ch
target: Individuals needing a flexible and easy-to-use communication solution.

description: __Hardware __ __ __ __ __ A variety of switches (e.g., Jelly Bean, Big Red) and mounting solutions to enable device access for individuals with motor impairments.

product: The Physical Bridge to Digital Autonomy
description: For adults with profound autism and motor challenges, the barrier to digital participation often lies in the physical precision required to touch a screen. AbleNet’s suite of access tools—anchored by industry standards like the **Jelly Bean** and **Big Red** switches—provides a ruggedized bridge between physical intent and digital output. Unlike standard consumer peripherals, these devices are built to **“ableSTRONG”** specifications, ensuring they can withstand the drops, moisture, and heavy force often associated with behavioral dysregulation or spasticity. The ecosystem has evolved beyond s
target: Individuals with physical disabilities who cannot use direct touch access.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ An iOS-based AAC device built on an iPad, known for its durability and a unique wearable, wireless speaker option.

product: The Wearable Voice: Reclaiming Social Attention
description: The **ProSlate** series (available in 4, 8, 10, and 13-inch models) tackles a subtle but profound barrier in adult AAC use: the “disemobodies voice.” Standard communication devices project sound from the tablet on a table or wheelchair mount, causing listeners to stare at the screen rather than the user. Forbes AAC solves this with the **SoundPOD** , a removable, wireless speaker module that can be worn on a lanyard around the user’s neck. This simple hardware shift redirects the listener’s attention to the user’s face, fostering natural eye contact and validating the user as the source of the
target: Users who prefer the iOS platform and a variety of communication app choices, with a need for a rugged solution.

description: __Software (iOS App) __ A symbol-based communication app that provides a voice to those who cannot speak, known for its extensive vocabulary levels and customization.

product: The App That Standardized the Voice
description: **Proloquo2Go** is the application that fundamentally shifted AAC from exclusive medical hardware to consumer devices, effectively democratizing access for families and adults who could not afford dedicated systems. Unlike its newer sibling app (simply called _Proloquo_ , which utilizes fixed layouts), Proloquo2Go remains the definitive choice for adults with complex fine-motor or visual challenges due to its deep customizability—supporting grid sizes from 9 to 144 buttons. Its core innovation, the **Crescendo** vocabulary system, offers a robust language set that avoids the “infantilization”
target: Children and adults with autism, Down syndrome, cerebral palsy, and other diagnoses.

description: __Software (iOS App) __ A text-based communication app designed for literate individuals who cannot speak, featuring word prediction and multi-language support.

product: The Literate Voice: Efficiency and Identity
description: For literate adults with profound autism or acquired speech loss (such as from ALS), symbol-based AAC often feels infantilizing and slow. **Proloquo4Text** addresses this by providing a text-to-speech interface designed specifically for speed and social nuance. The app utilizes **PolyPredix** , a self-learning multi-word prediction engine that anticipates not just the next word but entire phrases, reducing keystrokes by approximately 50%. Uniquely, it features a “single screen” layout that surrounds the typing area with customizable “Quick Talk” blocks, allowing users to interject rapid social
target: Literate individuals who need a voice output solution, including those with ALS or cerebral palsy.

description: __Software (iOS App) __ An official, licensed digital version of the Pragmatic Organisation Dynamic Display (PODD) communication books.

product: The Hybrid Approach: Bridging Digital and Paper Worlds
description: **simPODD** stands out in the AAC landscape as the only application that officially integrates Gayle Porter’s **Pragmatic Organisation Dynamic Display (PODD)** system in both digital and physical forms. For adults with profound autism who rely on the PODD methodology—which prioritizes social interaction and pragmatic “branching” (e.g., “I have something to say” → “It’s about an activity”) over simple noun naming—this app solves a critical logistical failure: the lack of consistent backups. Unlike other apps where the device is the only voice, simPODD features a built-in **printing engine**. Th
target: Users of the PODD communication system who want a digital, dynamic display option.

description: __Software __ Software that allows users to control a computer cursor with head movements via a standard webcam, without the need for additional hardware.

product: The Invisible Interface: Access Without Contact
description: For adults with profound autism and co-occurring motor challenges (such as apraxia or dyspraxia), the physical act of manipulating a mouse or touching a screen can be a barrier to digital autonomy. **ViVo Mouse** removes this friction by transforming a standard, low-cost webcam into a precise head-tracking controller. Unlike expensive proprietary eye-trackers, this software-first solution requires no wearable dots, headsets, or specialized hardware. It detects the user’s face and translates natural head movements into cursor control, effectively turning the user's nose into a pointer. A key fe
target: Individuals with limited or no use of their hands, including those with spinal cord injuries, ALS, and severe physical disabilities.

description: __Software __ A comprehensive software package for communication, computer access, and environmental control, supporting various access methods like touch, switch, and eye gaze.

product: The Operating System for Independent Living
description: **Grid 3** distinguishes itself not merely as a communication app, but as a comprehensive operating system for adult autonomy. While many AAC solutions focus solely on speech output, Grid 3 integrates **Computer Control** and **Environmental Control (ECU)** directly into the interface, acknowledging that for an adult with profound physical or intellectual disabilities, well-being requires the ability to interact with the world, not just talk about it. The software enables users to operate Windows applications (like Word or Excel) and smart home devices (lights, doors, televisions) using the sa
target: Individuals with a wide range of disabilities affecting speech, language, and motor skills.

description: __Speech-Generating Device (SGD) __ A range of dedicated Windows-based communication devices powered by Grid 3 software, available with various access methods including eye gaze.

product: The Social-First Device: Validating the User
description: The **Grid Pad** series (currently headlined by the **10s, 13, and 16** models) fundamentally redesigns the AAC form factor to prioritize social connection over mere data output. Its standout feature is the rear-facing **Partner Screen** , a full-color display that projects the user’s message (symbol, text, or emoji) to the listener. This simple hardware addition shifts the conversation dynamic from “staring at a computer” to natural face-to-face interaction, validating the user’s presence in a group setting. For adults with profound autism, this reduces the stigma of the “black box” barrier a
target: Users needing a complete and integrated AAC hardware and software solution.

description: __Software (Mobile App) __ Uses artificial intelligence to provide real-time, personalized feedback on a child's speech and pronunciation through interactive, game-based exercises on an iPad. These tools sit at the frontier of AI-enabled diagnosis, screening, and therapy. They tackle a real crisis—the shortage of human SLPs and early childhood specialists—while raising difficult questions about data use, consent, algorithmic bias, and whether AI should augment or replace human clinicians. AI for Education and Literacy
target: Children with speech sound disorders and articulation difficulties.

description: __Software (Mobile App) __ __ __ A free, AI-based reading tutor app that listens to children read aloud, offering real-time corrective feedback and pronunciation support to build literacy skills. It works offline and includes in-app games.

product: The Private Tutor: Literacy Without Judgment
description: **Google Read Along** (formerly Bolo) repurposes advanced speech recognition into a tool for literacy maintenance, a critical but often overlooked need for adults with intellectual disabilities who may experience skill regression after leaving school. Unlike standard text-to-speech tools that read _to_ the user, Read Along listens to the user read aloud, using an on-device AI agent named **Diya** to provide real-time, corrective feedback. For adults who may feel shame or anxiety about their reading levels, Diya offers a uniquely non-judgmental partner that never tires, allowing for high-repeti
target: Early readers and children learning to read.

description: __Software (Mobile App) __ __ Employs AI and video modeling to create an adaptive learning environment that encourages children to practice sounds and words, adjusting to their performance.

product: The Digital Mirror: Activating Speech Through Imitation
description: **Speech Blubs** utilizes the evidence-based practice of **peer video modeling** to bypass the cognitive barriers often associated with traditional speech therapy. For individuals with profound autism and apraxia, static images or cartoons often fail to trigger the brain's “mirror neurons”—the neural pathways responsible for imitation and empathy. Speech Blubs addresses this by filling the screen with real human faces (currently children) making specific sounds and mouth movements. This direct visual feedback loops encourages the user to mimic the action, a critical first step for adults who m
target: Children with speech delays, autism, and apraxia.

manufacturer: University of Kansas' Life Span Institute
description: __Research Project __ __ A research initiative using machine learning models to analyze recordings of children's vocalizations and play. The AI identifies vocal biomarkers and behavioral patterns to accelerate the identification of early communication milestones and potential developmental delays.

manufacturer: University of Kansas' Life Span Institute
description: **The AI-Powered Early Communication Assessment** (an initiative of the Juniper Gardens Children's Project) targets the single biggest failure point in the history of profound autism care: the diagnostic bottleneck. Historically, measuring a child’s “Early Communication Indicator” (ECI)—counting gestures, vocalizations, and eye gazes—required trained clinicians to painstakingly review hours of video, a scarcity that left many current adults with profound autism without intervention during their critical neuroplastic windows. This project uses machine learning to automate this coding, identifyi
target: Infants and toddlers for early developmental screening and autism risk assessment.

description: __Software (Web Widget) __ __ __ __ __ __ An AI tool that helps websites meet accessibility compliance standards (WCAG, ADA) by providing an interface for features like screen reading, contrast adjustment, and enhanced keyboard navigation, while also automating backend remediation.

product: Modulating the Sensory Web: A User-Controlled Layer
description: **UserWay** (acquired by digital accessibility leader Level Access in early 2024) operates on the premise that the modern web is often too visually chaotic for neurodivergent users. While best known for its automated compliance tools, the widget's most tangible value for adults with profound autism and sensory processing disorders lies in its **interface controls**. Users or caregivers can instantly "calm" a webpage by pausing distracting animations, enabling **Dyslexia-Friendly fonts** , and activating a "Reading Mask" to focus attention on a single line of text—features that directly reduce
target: Individuals with disabilities who need to access web content, including those with visual, motor, and cognitive impairments.

description: __Software (Web Widget) __ __ __ __ __ __ An AI-powered tool that scans websites to identify and automatically fix accessibility issues, improving the user experience for people with disabilities and helping site owners achieve compliance with standards like WCAG and ADA.

product: The Sensory Gatekeeper: AI as a Digital Filter
description: For adults with profound autism and sensory processing differences, the modern web's chaotic mix of auto-playing videos, flashing ads, and complex navigation can effectively lock them out of digital society. **EqualWeb** attempts to solve this not just through backend code fixes, but by giving the user direct control over their sensory experience via its **Auto AI Widget**. A user (or their caregiver) can activate specific **Accessibility Profiles** —such as "Cognitive Disability," "Epilepsy," or "ADHD"—which instantly strip away distractions, pause flashing animations, and activate a "Reading
target: Individuals with disabilities browsing the web, including those with visual, auditory, motor, and cognitive impairments.
